description: Jitterbug dance contest with Diane and grandparents; transitions to first-person POV falling onto pillow
notes: Memory/reality fragment that bookends the dream. Per analysis: 'innocent persona of past' that gets inserted into dream

description: Camilla in limo says 'We don't stop here'; assassination attempt foiled by car accident; she escapes injured and amnesiac
notes: In real timeline this is the limo ride to Adam's party; transformed in dream into assassination

description: Dan tells Herb about recurring dream of a man behind Winkie's; they investigate; Dan collapses in fear seeing the bum
notes: Pure dream-logic scene. Dan is a character Diane saw at Winkie's when meeting hitman

description: Wheelchair-bound Mr. Roque issues unspoken commands via phone chain about 'the girl'
notes: Hollywood power broker persona; symbolic of unyielding control

description: Betty arrives at LAX from Deep River Ontario, cheerfully says goodbye to elderly couple Irene and her husband who later laugh maniacally
notes: Innocent persona enters the dream world. Note the 'sinister laugh' foreshadowing

description: Injured amnesiac Rita walks down Sunset Boulevard at dawn, hides in bushes, sneaks into apartment
notes: Mirrors William Mulholland water flowing 'down hill into LA'

description: Detectives Harry and Neal investigate Mulholland Drive crash; find earring suggesting missing person
notes: Brief scene; detective figures recurring throughout

scene_title: Rita Hides in Aunt Ruths Apartment
description: Rita sneaks past aunt Ruth who is leaving for Canada, hides under coffee table then in bedroom
notes: Aunt Ruth's apartment becomes safe haven in dream

description: Coco welcomes Betty to courtyard apartment; Betty discovers Rita in shower; Rita claims her name is Rita seeing 'Gilda' poster
notes: Two key personas meet. Rita takes name from Rita Hayworth poster

description: Mob brothers force director Adam to cast 'this is the girl' Camilla Rhodes (blonde); Luigi spits espresso into napkin
notes: Critical 'this is the girl' line - parallels Mulholland's 'There it is, take it'

description: After Adam refuses, Mr. Roque orders 'Shut everything down'
notes: Suicide metaphor - 'shut everything down' is Diane contemplating suicide

description: Hitman Joe accidentally kills Ed and two bystanders trying to steal the black book containing prostitution call list
notes: Joe is the hitman Diane hires - revealed as comically incompetent in dream

scene_title: Betty Calls Aunt Ruth / Rita Wakes
description: Betty pretends to Aunt Ruth that all is well, decides to help Rita find her identity
notes: Betty embraces investigation 'like in the movies'

description: Adam comes home, finds wife Lorraine in bed with pool man Gene; pours pink paint in her jewelry box
notes: Adam getting humiliated mirrors Diane's humiliation

description: Adam learns from secretary Cynthia he's been fired and is broke
notes: Adam loses everything - dream punishment

description: Betty and Rita see news about accident; phone tracing scene; phone call to D. Hingle (Diane Selwyn)
notes: Phone call to Diane's actual apartment from dream

description: The Cowboy delivers cryptic ultimatum: 'You will see me one more time if you do good. You will see me two more times if you do bad'
notes: Mythic figure - represents Diane's attempt at smarter persona

description: Rita reveals to Betty huge amount of cash and a strange blue key in her purse
notes: Blue key central symbol introduced - signifies Camilla's death in real world

description: Betty does shockingly powerful audition with sleazy actor Woody for Wally's project; impresses casting director Linney
notes: Reveals childhood sexual abuse subtext: 'I hate you. I hate us both!' - script implies abuse by father's friend

scene_title: Set of The Sylvia North Story
description: Linney brings Betty to Adam's set; Adam locks eyes with Betty; Camilla Rhodes (blonde) lip-syncs '16 Reasons'
notes: Adam choosing the wrong Camilla - dream revenge for real-world rejection

description: Betty and Rita investigate Diane Selwyn's apartment, knock on door of #17 then #12
notes: Apartments switched - this is the swap with neighbor

description: Betty and Rita find rotting corpse on bed - Diane's dead body, foreshadowing the suicide ending
notes: Same bed as opening pillow shot. Diane sees herself dead

description: Rita transforms into Betty's double with blonde wig; they make love
notes: Rita merges with Betty - identity dissolving

description: Magician declares 'No hay banda - it is all an illusion'; Rebekah del Rio sings 'Llorando' (Crying); Betty finds blue box in purse
notes: Climactic revelation scene - 'It's all illusion'. del Rio collapses while singing continues

description: Betty disappears as Rita opens blue box; box falls empty; Aunt Ruth checks empty room
notes: Dream ends. Betty/Diane personality dissolves. Blue box = locked truth

description: The Cowboy says 'Hey pretty girl, time to wake up'; Diane wakes wearing same robe
notes: Reality begins. Same pillow as opening fall. Cowboy's 'one more time if good'

description: Diane sees Camilla on couch then realizes hallucination; grumpy neighbor returns to retrieve dishes
notes: Lynch's clue #6: notice robe, ashtray, coffee cup

description: Flashback: Camilla kisses Adam on set in front of Diane; humiliation
notes: The real moment of betrayal

description: Camilla breaks up with Diane on couch / Diane forces herself on Camilla
notes: Forced sexual encounter / breakup

description: Diane masturbates while crying; bitter emotion
notes: Self-destructive scene. Lynch clue: 'watch what Diane is wearing'

description: Phone rings - Camilla invites Diane to Adam's house party. Limo arrives
notes: Lynch clue #2: Notice red lampshade. Limo ride that becomes the dream's opening

description: Diane in limo, says 'We don't stop here'; Camilla appears, leads her up secret path
notes: Same words as dream limo scene; this is the actual event

description: Diane meets Adam's mom Coco; Camilla and the blonde Camilla kiss; engagement announcement; the Cowboy walks past; Diane breaks down crying
notes: All the dream personas seen as real people; engagement is the trigger

description: Diane hires hitman Joe; he says 'when it's done you'll find this' showing blue key; Dan looks over
notes: This is what triggers Dan's dream sequence at start of film

scene_title: Diane Returns to Apartment / Sees Key
description: Diane sits on couch, sees blue key on coffee table - the murder is done
notes: Realization moment - Camilla is actually dead

description: Old couple emerge as demons from blue box; chase Diane to bedroom; she shoots herself in head; smoke appears
notes: Suicide with gun. Demons from blue box represent abusive past

description: Final image: Betty and Rita appear over LA skyline; blue-haired lady whispers 'Silencio'
notes: Closing word. Hamlet's 'rest is silence'. End
